findings.evidence: `rg` shows the existing local convention: `AbelJacobi.lean:69 lemma weightedAbelJacobiClass_base ... S.weightedAbelJacobiClass w hdeg hx₀ x₀ = 0` and `AbelJacobi.lean:113 lemma unweightedAbelJacobiClass_base ... S.unweightedAbelJacobiClass hdeg x₀ x₀ = 0`, while the new declaration at `BasepointChange.lean:108-111` evaluates the `hy₀`-based class at `x₀`.
findings.fix: Rename this theorem to make the evaluated old point explicit, e.g. `weightedAbelJacobiClass_oldBase_eq_basepointChangeClass`, and update its use at line 121.
findings.issue: `weightedAbelJacobiClass_base_eq_basepointChangeClass` says `S.weightedAbelJacobiClass w hdeg hy₀ x₀ = ...`, so the actual Abel-Jacobi base point is `y₀`; local `_base` names mean evaluation at the chosen base point.

summary: One introduced theorem name reuses the local `_base` convention for a point that is not the Abel-Jacobi base point, making the API direction misleading.
